content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate URL aliases, byte-identical responses, equivalent admitted records, and replayed observations without deleting provenance.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Exact Duplicate Policy model · Theory Exact Dupl…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Exact Duplicate Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Exact Duplicate Policy model · Theory Exact Dupl…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 9257 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate stage digests, exact identity, near-duplicate review, change classes, versions, and tombstones; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate URL aliases, byte-identical responses, equivalent admitted records, and replayed observations without deleting provenance.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Exact Duplicate Policy model · Theory Exact Dupl…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Exact content duplicates have no independent audit value.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-ts-exact-duplicate-policy-1x9lye.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-ts-exact-duplicate-policy-1x9lye(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_crawler_ts_exact_duplicate_policy_1x9lye\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?observationCases < 15)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?classifiedCases < 15 \|\| classifiedCases > observationCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?changedVersions < 6)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?falseMerges > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using separate and aliases plus specific case evidence.
